test.name: Correct a transcription typo in a Birth fact's date — real mutation with minimality
test.description: The mid-research-flynn-typo scenario seeds tree.gedcomx.json with a day-swap typo: Patrick Flynn's F2.date reads `1908-03-21`, but the cited source S3 (Pennsylvania Death Certificate no. 4521) records 12 March 1908. This is the canonical 'ad-hoc edit' case from SKILL.md §'Correcting a value': edit is verifiable against an already-cited source, so the threshold is met. Tests the edit-minimality dimension under a REAL mutation (the other no-op tests ut_001/002 never exercise mutation paths). Only F2.date must change — every other byte of both project files must be unchanged.

input.user_message: tree-edit: change birth year — actually, fix this date. Patrick Flynn's death fact F2 currently shows `1908-03-21` in the tree, but the death certificate (S3, cert. no. 4521) records 12 March 1908. The tree has a day-swap typo. Correct F2.date to `1908-03-12` and leave everything else alone.

judge_context: Should call validate_research_schema AFTER the edit — SKILL.md §Validation requires post-edit validation (a pre-edit call is optional and not required by the SKILL.md). Judges must NOT score down for skipping a pre-edit validation call
Should change ONLY F2.date in tree.gedcomx.json from `1908-03-21` to `1908-03-12`

Edit-minimality REQUIRED: no other field on F2 may change (place, sources, type, primary all stay byte-identical); no other fact (F1, F3) is touched; no other person (I2) is touched; no relationship (R1) is touched; research.json is not touched at all
Should NOT 'normalize' adjacent fields opportunistically (e.g., re-resolving F2.place via place_search when only the date was wrong)
Source-grounding requirement (per evidence-grounded-edits.md): the correction must be tied to S3 — the user cited it explicitly. The skill should reflect that in the explanation
Should report what was changed and confirm post-edit schema validity in the response
